statement.markdown: Given a sequence of parentheses, determine whether the parentheses are balanced or not. A balanced string has all its matching parentheses in the correct order.
statement.inputFormat: A string containing a sequence of parentheses
statement.outputFormat: (boolean) true if the parentheses are balanced, false otherwise
statement.notes: You should use a stack to implement the solution

hints.text: You should use a stack to keep track of the opening parentheses

editorial.title: Stack Implementation: Balanced Parentheses
editorial.intuition: You can use a stack to keep track of the opening parentheses. When you encounter a closing parenthesis, you pop the top element from the stack and check if it matches the closing parenthesis.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use two pointers to iterate over the string and check if each closing parenthesis matches the corresponding opening parenthesis.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use a stack to keep track of the opening parentheses. When you encounter a closing parenthesis, you pop the top element from the stack and check if it matches the closing parenthesis.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: You can use mathematical induction to prove that the stack implementation correctly identifies balanced parentheses.
editorial.dryRun: You can use a dry run to test your implementation with sample inputs.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle edge cases such as empty strings
